CAT01-030.headline: Self-Awareness Without Self-Judgment
CAT01-030.subtitle: The role of self-compassion
CAT01-030.figureLine: A single seated clay figure cradles one hand softly against its own chest, posture open and unguarded, conveying honest noticing held with warmth rather than blame.
CAT01-030.sceneLine: A soft amber accent glow rests gently in the figure's cupped palm, edges warm and unhurried, suggesting awareness met with kindness instead of criticism.

CAT03-014.headline: How To Say No
CAT03-014.subtitle: Scripts and strategies for setting limits
CAT03-014.figureLine: A single standing clay figure raises one open palm in a calm, steady gesture, posture grounded and unhurried, conveying a boundary held without apology.
CAT03-014.sceneLine: A clear line of soft light amber forms a gentle curved threshold between the figure and the space beyond, suggesting a limit drawn calmly and respected.

CAT04-002.headline: Where Self-Esteem Comes From
CAT04-002.subtitle: Childhood origins and development
CAT04-002.figureLine: A single seated clay figure leans down to tend a small rounded form near its feet, posture protective and patient, conveying worth taking root from its earliest beginnings.
CAT04-002.sceneLine: A slender amber accent shoot rises from the ground beside the figure, base broad and steady, suggesting a sense of self growing from deep early roots.

CAT04-004.headline: The Self-Esteem Spectrum
CAT04-004.subtitle: Fragile, contingent, and stable
CAT04-004.figureLine: A single standing clay figure rests with feet planted wide and weight settled low, posture steady and unshakable, conveying worth that holds firm under pressure.
CAT04-004.sceneLine: Three soft amber forms sit beside the figure, one wobbling on a point, one balanced on a slope, one resting flat and stable, suggesting different ways self-worth can rest.

CAT04-005.headline: Why Affirmations Backfire
CAT04-005.subtitle: Positive self-talk and low self-esteem
CAT04-005.figureLine: A single seated clay figure turns its head gently aside from a bright shape held out toward it, posture honest and unconvinced, conveying words that do not yet fit.
CAT04-005.sceneLine: A polished amber accent form floats just out of reach beside the figure, surface too smooth to grip, suggesting affirmations that slide off rather than land.

CAT04-007.headline: Why Achievement Cannot Build Self-Worth
CAT04-007.subtitle: The limits of accomplishment
CAT04-007.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands beside a tall stack of forms yet keeps one hand pressed to its own chest, posture searching and unsatisfied, conveying worth not found in the pile.
CAT04-007.sceneLine: A column of soft light amber rounded forms rises high beside the figure yet casts a hollow gap at its base, suggesting accomplishment that leaves the foundation unfilled.

CAT04-009.headline: How Gender Shapes Self-Esteem
CAT04-009.subtitle: Self-worth and societal expectations
CAT04-009.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands tall while gently easing a draped form off its shoulders, posture freeing and self-possessed, conveying worth claimed beyond outside expectation.
CAT04-009.sceneLine: A loose amber accent cloth lifts away from the figure's shoulders on a soft draft, edges releasing, suggesting imposed expectations gradually loosening their hold.

CAT04-010.headline: Measuring Self-Esteem
CAT04-010.subtitle: Validated tools for tracking self-worth
CAT04-010.figureLine: A single seated clay figure holds two rounded forms in level, open palms, posture attentive and calm, conveying steady reflection on how one regards oneself.
CAT04-010.sceneLine: A gentle pair of soft light amber forms rests in balance on a low beam beside the figure, suggesting careful, honest measurement held without judgment.

CAT04-011.headline: Self-Compassion
CAT04-011.subtitle: Kristin Neff's three core components
CAT04-011.figureLine: A single seated clay figure wraps both arms gently around itself in a soft, steady hold, posture tender and complete, conveying kindness turned inward.
CAT04-011.sceneLine: Three soft amber accent rings rest loosely interlinked beside the figure, each open and unbroken, suggesting kindness, common humanity, and mindfulness held together.

CAT04-012.headline: Where The Inner Critic Comes From
CAT04-012.subtitle: Reducing the power of a harsh inner voice
CAT04-012.figureLine: A single seated clay figure turns calmly toward a small shadowed shape at its shoulder, posture composed and curious, conveying a harsh inner voice met without fear.
CAT04-012.sceneLine: A dim form at the figure's shoulder softens as soft light amber spreads gently around it, suggesting the inner critic losing its sharp grip.

CAT04-013.headline: Helpful Vs Harmful Self-Talk
CAT04-013.subtitle: Changing the patterns we repeat
CAT04-013.figureLine: A single seated clay figure gently redirects one rounded form away from a tangled cluster toward a smoother path, posture deliberate and patient, conveying old patterns being reshaped.
CAT04-013.sceneLine: A knotted amber accent strand beside the figure eases into a smoother soft light amber line as it travels onward, suggesting harsh self-talk reworked into something kinder.

CAT04-014.headline: Self-Compassion When Undeserving
CAT04-014.subtitle: Practicing kindness when worth feels distant
CAT04-014.figureLine: A single seated clay figure lets one hand rest open on its own knee while the other reaches tentatively toward a warm shape, posture hesitant yet softening, conveying kindness offered even when unfelt.
CAT04-014.sceneLine: A small pale amber glow lingers patiently near the figure's open hand, dim but steady, suggesting worth that remains present even when it feels out of reach.

CAT04-015.headline: The Self-Compassion Break
CAT04-015.subtitle: A three-minute practice for hard moments
CAT04-015.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests both palms lightly over its heart with shoulders easing downward, posture pausing and settled, conveying a brief, grounding moment of care.
CAT04-015.sceneLine: A soft amber accent ripple radiates outward in three calm rings from the figure's chest, suggesting a short, steadying practice spreading warmth in a hard moment.

CAT04-016.headline: Common Humanity
CAT04-016.subtitle: Struggle is part of being human
CAT04-016.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands with one hand extended openly toward distant smaller forms, posture connected and unalone, conveying shared struggle held in common.
CAT04-016.sceneLine: A continuous soft light amber thread loops gently from the figure outward to several faint forms beyond, suggesting the quiet link between one person's struggle and everyone's.

CAT04-021.headline: Imposter Syndrome
CAT04-021.subtitle: Why successful people feel like frauds
CAT04-021.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands atop a solid platform yet glances back hesitantly at its own steady footing, posture capable but doubting, conveying real competence felt as fraud.
CAT04-021.sceneLine: A firm amber accent platform holds the figure securely while a faint shadow of doubt flickers beneath it, suggesting genuine standing that feels unearned.

CAT04-022.headline: Five Types Of Imposter Syndrome
CAT04-022.subtitle: How to recognize the patterns
CAT04-022.figureLine: A single standing clay figure turns slowly to consider five distinct forms arranged around it, posture observant and reflective, conveying recognition of familiar self-doubt patterns.
CAT04-022.sceneLine: Five soft light amber forms of differing shapes encircle the figure at an even distance, suggesting the distinct faces self-doubt can take.

CAT04-025.headline: How Perfectionism Fuels Imposter Syndrome
CAT04-025.subtitle: The link between flawless and fraudulent
CAT04-025.figureLine: A single seated clay figure smooths a rounded form over and over yet keeps glancing away unsatisfied, posture meticulous and uneasy, conveying impossible standards feeding self-doubt.
CAT04-025.sceneLine: An over-polished amber accent form beside the figure reflects a restless shimmer, never quite settling, suggesting perfectionism that keeps fraud-feelings alive.

CAT04-026.headline: Imposter Syndrome And Systemic Barriers
CAT04-026.subtitle: Self-doubt in marginalized groups
CAT04-026.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands firmly while leaning against a subtle weight at its side, posture resilient and clear-eyed, conveying doubt shaped by outside barriers, not lack of worth.
CAT04-026.sceneLine: A soft light amber form holds steady against a heavier dim mass pressing from one side of the figure, suggesting external barriers met with quiet endurance.

CAT04-027.headline: Chronic Self-Doubt
CAT04-027.subtitle: Why decision-making becomes paralyzing
CAT04-027.figureLine: A single standing clay figure pauses mid-step between two diverging paths, one foot lifted and held, posture caught and hesitant, conveying decisions stalled by doubt.
CAT04-027.sceneLine: Two soft amber accent paths fork gently before the figure, each fading into uncertainty, suggesting choices that feel impossible to commit to.

CAT04-028.headline: Overcoming Imposter Syndrome
CAT04-028.subtitle: Evidence-based strategies that help
CAT04-028.figureLine: A single standing clay figure plants both hands firmly on a solid form before it, posture grounding and resolved, conveying doubt steadily met with proven steps.
CAT04-028.sceneLine: A series of soft light amber footholds rises in a gentle, reliable stair beside the figure, suggesting practical strategies that build firm ground underfoot.

CAT04-029.headline: Dunning-Kruger Vs Imposter Syndrome
CAT04-029.subtitle: Opposite failures of self-assessment
CAT04-029.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds two mismatched forms at uneven heights, one too large and one too small, posture weighing and discerning, conveying the gap between felt and actual ability.
CAT04-029.sceneLine: Two amber accent forms sit on a tilted beam beside the figure, one swollen and one shrunken, suggesting opposite distortions of how we judge ourselves.

CAT04-030.headline: Rewriting The Imposter Narrative
CAT04-030.subtitle: From fraud to competent
CAT04-030.figureLine: A single seated clay figure reshapes a rough form between its palms into a clearer, steadier shape, posture intent and assured, conveying a self-story being remade.
CAT04-030.sceneLine: A jagged amber accent form smooths into a confident soft light amber curve in the figure's hands, suggesting a fraud-story rewritten into one of competence.

CAT04-041.headline: Identity Formation
CAT04-041.subtitle: Development across the lifespan
CAT04-041.figureLine: A single standing clay figure walks forward along a gentle rise, head lifted and posture evolving, conveying a self that takes shape and deepens over a lifetime.
CAT04-041.sceneLine: A continuous soft light amber line winds upward beside the figure, gathering form as it rises, suggesting identity composing itself across the years.

CAT04-042.headline: Identity Crises
CAT04-042.subtitle: Understanding and navigating them
CAT04-042.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands at a fork between scattered forms yet keeps its center calm and upright, posture searching but steady, conveying a self being questioned without coming apart.
CAT04-042.sceneLine: Loose amber accent fragments drift around the figure then begin drawing back toward a single soft light amber center, suggesting a fractured sense of self gathering again.

CAT04-043.headline: Living Authentically
CAT04-043.subtitle: What it means and why it is hard
CAT04-043.figureLine: A single standing clay figure steps forward with chest open and arms relaxed at its sides, posture unmasked and at ease, conveying a self shown plainly and without disguise.
CAT04-043.sceneLine: A clear soft amber accent light falls evenly over the figure with no shadow held back, suggesting a self lived openly in full view.

CAT04-044.headline: Clarifying Values
CAT04-044.subtitle: Guiding life decisions and direction
CAT04-044.figureLine: A single standing clay figure faces a single steady point on the horizon, one hand raised to shade its view, posture oriented and purposeful, conveying direction drawn from core values.
CAT04-044.sceneLine: A small steady amber accent point glows on the horizon ahead of the figure, suggesting clear values that guide each choice forward.

CAT04-045.headline: When Masking Erodes Identity
CAT04-045.subtitle: Code-switching and people-pleasing
CAT04-045.figureLine: A single standing clay figure begins easing a thin covering away from its form, posture relieved and reclaiming, conveying a true self emerging from constant adjustment.
CAT04-045.sceneLine: A faint amber accent veil thins and lifts from the figure to reveal a warmer soft light amber form beneath, suggesting an authentic self surfacing from behind the mask.

CAT04-047.headline: Gender Identity And Mental Health
CAT04-047.subtitle: Understanding the research
CAT04-047.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands tall and at home in its own form, one hand resting calmly over its center, posture settled and self-affirming, conveying a self fully inhabited.
CAT04-047.sceneLine: A warm soft amber accent glow rises evenly from within the figure's outline, suggesting an identity held with steadiness and dignity.

CAT04-048.headline: Sexual Orientation Self-Acceptance
CAT04-048.subtitle: The coming out process
CAT04-048.figureLine: A single standing clay figure gently opens both arms outward from its chest toward the light ahead, posture emerging and unafraid, conveying a true self stepping into the open.
CAT04-048.sceneLine: A soft light amber dawn-glow widens gradually before the figure as it moves toward it, suggesting acceptance unfolding into openness.

CAT04-049.headline: Career Over-Identification
CAT04-049.subtitle: When work becomes the whole identity
CAT04-049.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands fused to a single tall form yet turns to notice the wider space around it, posture realizing and loosening, conveying a self larger than its work.
CAT04-049.sceneLine: A narrow amber accent column beside the figure softens at its edges to reveal a broader soft light amber field around it, suggesting an identity wider than any single role.

CAT04-051.headline: The Confidence Gap
CAT04-051.subtitle: Acting despite self-doubt
CAT04-051.figureLine: A single standing clay figure steps across a small gap toward the next ground, mid-stride and committed, posture brave and forward, conveying action taken before certainty arrives.
CAT04-051.sceneLine: A soft amber accent bridge of light spans a narrow break in the ground beneath the figure's leading foot, suggesting forward movement made despite the doubt.

CAT04-052.headline: Self-Efficacy
CAT04-052.subtitle: How believing in yourself shapes achievement
CAT04-052.figureLine: A single standing clay figure reaches upward with a sure, extended arm toward a form just within grasp, posture confident and capable, conveying belief turning into reach.
CAT04-052.sceneLine: A soft light amber form glows brighter the closer the figure's hand draws to it, suggesting self-belief widening what feels possible.

CAT04-053.headline: How Small Wins Build Confidence
CAT04-053.subtitle: Lasting confidence and motivation
CAT04-053.figureLine: A single standing clay figure sets a small rounded form atop a low, growing cluster, posture steady and encouraged, conveying confidence built one modest step at a time.
CAT04-053.sceneLine: A gentle stack of soft amber accent forms grows taller and warmer beside the figure with each added piece, suggesting small wins accumulating into lasting confidence.

CAT04-054.headline: Productive Discomfort
CAT04-054.subtitle: Finding the learning zone beyond comfort
CAT04-054.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans just past the edge of a settled ring into open space, posture stretching and willing, conveying growth found at the edge of comfort.
CAT04-054.sceneLine: A soft amber accent ring around the figure gives way to a warmer glowing band just beyond it, suggesting the learning zone that lies past the comfortable center.

CAT04-055.headline: How Social Comparison Harms Wellbeing
CAT04-055.subtitle: And how to stop measuring against others
CAT04-055.figureLine: A single standing clay figure turns its gaze away from a row of distant forms back toward its own ground, posture refocusing and self-returning, conveying attention reclaimed from comparison.
CAT04-055.sceneLine: A line of distant amber accent forms dims as a warmer soft light amber glow gathers close around the figure itself, suggesting attention drawn back from others to one's own path.

CAT04-056.headline: Rebuilding Confidence After Failure
CAT04-056.subtitle: Finding footing again
CAT04-056.figureLine: A single clay figure rises slowly from a low crouch, one hand pressing against the ground for support, posture recovering and rising, conveying confidence gathered back after a fall.
CAT04-056.sceneLine: A soft amber accent glow gathers beneath the rising figure's hand and steadies upward, suggesting footing returning after a setback.

CAT04-058.headline: Building Social Confidence
CAT04-058.subtitle: Connection-focused skills
CAT04-058.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans gently toward a nearby smaller form with one hand open in greeting, posture warm and approachable, conveying confidence grown through connection.
CAT04-058.sceneLine: A soft light amber thread reaches gently from the figure's open hand toward a nearby form, suggesting social ease built through genuine connection.

CAT04-059.headline: Body Language And Posture
CAT04-059.subtitle: How they influence confidence and mood
CAT04-059.figureLine: A single standing clay figure draws its shoulders back and lifts its chest open and upright, posture expanding and assured, conveying confidence shaped by how the body holds itself.
CAT04-059.sceneLine: A soft amber accent glow widens and brightens around the figure's opened, upright form, suggesting posture lifting both confidence and mood.

CAT04-060.headline: Confidence Vs Arrogance
CAT04-060.subtitle: Understanding the key differences
CAT04-060.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands tall yet keeps its stance grounded and open toward others, posture self-assured but unpuffed, conveying confidence held without overreach.
CAT04-060.sceneLine: A warm soft amber accent glow rests evenly within the figure's grounded outline rather than flaring outward, suggesting confidence that is steady, not inflated.

CAT04-061.headline: What Perfectionism Really Is
CAT04-061.subtitle: High standards versus self-punishment
CAT04-061.figureLine: A single seated clay figure works carefully on a form yet lays down a sharp implement it had gripped too tightly, posture exacting yet easing, conveying high standards loosened from self-punishment.
CAT04-061.sceneLine: A bright amber accent form beside the figure softens its hard, cutting edge into a warmer rounded glow, suggesting standards kept while harshness is set aside.

CAT04-062.headline: The Three Types Of Perfectionism
CAT04-062.subtitle: Their different consequences
CAT04-062.figureLine: A single standing clay figure considers three forms set at different distances and angles around it, posture discerning and aware, conveying distinct kinds of perfectionism recognized.
CAT04-062.sceneLine: Three soft light amber forms pull in three directions around the figure, one inward, one outward, one upward, suggesting the different shapes perfectionism takes.

CAT04-063.headline: How Perfectionism Leads To Burnout
CAT04-063.subtitle: Procrastination, burnout, and depression
CAT04-063.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits with shoulders bowed beneath a heavy form it had tried to make flawless, posture worn and weighted, conveying impossible standards draining strength.
CAT04-063.sceneLine: A dimming amber accent glow presses low over the figure while a faint warmer light waits patiently at the edge, suggesting heaviness held without despair.

CAT04-064.headline: The Good Enough Principle
CAT04-064.subtitle: Why perfectionism backfires
CAT04-064.figureLine: A single standing clay figure steps back from a finished form with hands relaxed and open, posture releasing and content, conveying a thing accepted as complete.
CAT04-064.sceneLine: A softly rounded, unpolished soft light amber form glows warmly beside the figure exactly as it is, suggesting good enough embraced over flawless.

CAT04-065.headline: People-Pleasing
CAT04-065.subtitle: The cost of saying yes to everyone
CAT04-065.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans far toward many reaching forms while its own ground thins beneath it, posture overextended and depleting, conveying the cost of endless yes.
CAT04-065.sceneLine: Many faint amber accent threads pull the figure outward in every direction, thinning the warm glow at its own center, suggesting a self stretched thin by constant giving.

CAT04-070.headline: Recovery From Perfectionism
CAT04-070.subtitle: What it actually looks like
CAT04-070.figureLine: A single standing clay figure rests one hand calmly on an imperfect form and leaves it unchanged, posture peaceful and accepting, conveying ease found beyond flawlessness.
CAT04-070.sceneLine: A gently uneven soft amber accent form glows warmly beside the figure with its rough edges intact, suggesting recovery that welcomes imperfection.

CAT06-019.headline: The Inner Child
CAT06-019.subtitle: Understanding and healing past emotional wounds
CAT06-019.figureLine: A single seated clay figure curves protectively around a smaller rounded form held softly against its chest, posture gentle and caring, conveying a tender part of the self being held safely.
CAT06-019.sceneLine: A warm soft amber accent glow surrounds the small form cradled by the figure, calm and protective, suggesting an early self met with care rather than the old hurt.

CAT06-020.headline: Reparenting Yourself
CAT06-020.subtitle: Meeting unmet childhood needs as an adult
CAT06-020.figureLine: A single clay figure kneels and extends a steady, open hand toward a smaller form beside it, posture nurturing and reassuring, conveying needs once unmet now answered with care.
CAT06-020.sceneLine: A soft light amber glow passes gently from the figure's open hand to the smaller form, suggesting steady care reaching what once went without.

CAT08-005.headline: Keystone Habits
CAT08-005.subtitle: How one change creates a chain reaction
CAT08-005.figureLine: A single standing clay figure sets one central form firmly in place, posture deliberate and grounding, conveying a single change that steadies everything around it.
CAT08-005.sceneLine: A single amber accent form clicks into place and several soft light amber forms settle into alignment around it, suggesting one keystone habit shifting the whole.

CAT08-006.headline: Habit Stacking
CAT08-006.subtitle: Attaching new behaviors to existing routines
CAT08-006.figureLine: A single standing clay figure carefully sets a new rounded form atop an existing steady one, posture methodical and assured, conveying a new habit anchored to an old one.
CAT08-006.sceneLine: A fresh soft amber accent form rests securely on top of an established pale amber base beside the figure, suggesting new behavior built onto a reliable routine.

CAT08-008.headline: Why Willpower Fails
CAT08-008.subtitle: Reducing friction works instead
CAT08-008.figureLine: A single standing clay figure lets a form roll smoothly down a cleared, gentle slope rather than straining to push it uphill, posture relieved and efficient, conveying ease over effort.
CAT08-008.sceneLine: A cleared soft light amber path slopes gently before the figure with obstacles swept aside, suggesting friction removed so change flows on its own.

CAT08-015.headline: Action Comes Before Motivation
CAT08-015.subtitle: Why movement precedes the urge
CAT08-015.figureLine: A single standing clay figure takes one deliberate first step and a warmth begins to gather around its motion, posture initiating and committed, conveying motivation following the act.
CAT08-015.sceneLine: A soft amber accent glow kindles around the figure's leading foot only once it moves, brightening with each step, suggesting drive that arrives after action begins.

CAT08-018.headline: Why Autonomy Matters
CAT08-018.subtitle: Self-determination and motivation
CAT08-018.figureLine: A single standing clay figure chooses its own direction with a free, unhurried stride, posture self-led and willing, conveying motivation that flows from genuine choice.
CAT08-018.sceneLine: A soft light amber path opens freely in the direction the figure itself turns toward, suggesting drive that grows from self-chosen direction.

CAT08-024.headline: Sustainable Self-Care
CAT08-024.subtitle: What it actually looks like
CAT08-024.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests in an easy, balanced posture with hands settled calmly in its lap, posture replenished and steady, conveying care that lasts rather than depletes.
CAT08-024.sceneLine: A steady soft amber accent glow refills gently and evenly around the resting figure, never flaring or fading, suggesting care that sustains over time.

CAT08-035.headline: Rewiring Negative Self-Talk
CAT08-035.subtitle: How to recognize and change the pattern
CAT08-035.figureLine: A single seated clay figure gently untangles a knotted strand and lays it into a smoother line, posture patient and attentive, conveying a harsh inner pattern being reworked.
CAT08-035.sceneLine: A tangled amber accent strand beside the figure resolves into a calm, continuous soft light amber line, suggesting negative self-talk rewired into something steadier.

CAT10-002.headline: The Social Media Comparison Trap
CAT10-002.subtitle: Its effect on self-esteem
CAT10-002.figureLine: A single standing clay figure turns away from a wall of bright distant forms back toward its own quiet glow, posture withdrawing and self-recentering, conveying worth reclaimed from comparison.
CAT10-002.sceneLine: A dazzling lattice of far-off amber accent forms dims as a warm soft light amber glow gathers close around the figure itself, suggesting self-worth drawn back from the feed.
